whatItDoes: Visual text diff comparison tool with side-by-side and inline modes. Highlights additions in green and deletions in red using an LCS-based diff algorithm. Shows line numbers and provides diff statistics (added, removed, unchanged lines).
whyItExists: Comparing two text files or code snippets visually is essential for code review, documentation updates, and content editing; this tool provides a quick, client-side diff comparison without needing command-line tools.
whoShouldUse: Developers comparing code changes, technical writers reviewing document edits, QA testers verifying configuration changes, and anyone who needs to visually compare two pieces of text.
useCases: Compare two versions of a code snippet to identify changes
Review configuration file modifications before deployment
Verify that a refactoring preserved all original functionality
Compare API responses before and after a change
Visualize changes between two similar text blocks
instructions: Paste the original text in the left textarea
Paste the modified text in the right textarea
Choose between Side by Side or Inline view mode
Green highlighted lines show additions, red shows deletions
Use the Copy Diff button to copy the unified diff output
Review the stats bar for a quick count of changes
examples: Compare two versions of a README.md file to see documentation changes
Diff a JSON configuration before and after adding new environment variables
Compare two SQL queries to find the differences in logic
bestPractices: Use Side by Side mode for comparing large blocks of text where changes are far apart
Use Inline mode for smaller diffs where you want to see changes in context
Paste clean text without extra whitespace for more accurate diff results
The diff operates on full lines — for word-level changes, compare individual paragraphs
Copy the diff output for use in commit messages or code review comments
commonMistakes: Expecting word-level highlighting — this tool operates at the line level
Including trailing whitespace that creates false positive differences
Comparing binary content or files with different encodings
Assuming the tool handles merge conflicts — it only shows differences
Not accounting for line ending differences (\r\n vs \n)
faq: What diff algorithm is used? — The tool uses a Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) based algorithm to compute the minimum number of additions and deletions.
Does it handle large files? — The tool works best with text under a few hundred lines. Very large files may slow down the browser.
Can I diff binary files? — No, this tool is designed for text comparison only.
What does the Copy Diff button copy? — It copies a unified diff format with + for additions, - for deletions, and space for unchanged lines.
